message:
fix(websocket): reject reserved close codes and 1-byte close payloads per RFC 6455

Frame::close: Add assertion rejecting close codes 1005, 1006, and 1015
per RFC 6455 §7.4.1 — these status codes are designated for use in APIs
and close frame analysis but MUST NOT be sent in a Close frame on the
wire. Panics with a clear message identifying the offending code.

Decoder: Add validation for Close frame payload length. Per RFC 6455
§5.5.1, a Close frame body must be either 0 bytes (no status code) or
at least 2 bytes (status code optionally followed by reason). A 1-byte
Close payload is structurally invalid and now returns
WsError::InvalidClosePayload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
